title: Surgical complication screening prompts
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

investigations.text: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.
investigations.rationale: Supports review of wound infection signs and systemic features for surgical complication screening prompts; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.

source.title: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment — Recommendations

investigations.text: Supports recovery observation, pain or nausea assessment, discharge criteria, instructions, escort, complications, handover, and follow-up only as clinician-completed documentation.
investigations.rationale: Supports documented recovery, complications, discharge and follow-up in licensed day-surgery services for surgical complication screening prompts; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports recovery observation, pain or nausea assessment, discharge criteria, instructions, escort, complications, handover, and follow-up only as clinician-completed documentation.

sources_used.title: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment — Recommendations

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Procedure-specific bleeding, thromboembolism, organ injury, leak, obstruction, urinary, respiratory, cardiac and neurological complications and action thresholds remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for surgical complication screening prompts.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
